label: Michigan Consumer Sentiment (Final)
deltaNote: vs Feb 2026 (56.6); year-ahead inflation expectations at 3.8% (largest monthly jump since Apr 2025)

label: VIX (Market Fear Index)
delta: Brief dip to ~18–20 (Apr 7 Iran ceasefire rally); spiking again Apr 9 on China 104%/84% dual tariff shock
deltaNote: US-Iran ceasefire (Apr 7) briefly pulled VIX toward 18–20; April 9 China tariff escalation to 104%/84% pushed fear index back above 29. Elevated uncertainty: Iran ceasefire fragile (Lebanon strikes continue), FOMC Apr 29–30 critical decision point

label: US Avg Effective Tariff Rate
contestNote: Apr 9: additional 50% tariff on China brings total US rate on Chinese goods to ~104% — highest since 1930s Smoot-Hawley era. Combined with Apr 2 metal expansion (50%) and pending pharma tariffs (100%), overall effective rate reaches highest since 1940s

label: China GDP Forecast 2025
contestNote: Down 0.6pp from Jan 2025 forecast; property crisis, tariff headwinds weigh on growth

label: Global Policy Uncertainty Index
source: Baker-Bloom-Davis / FRED; JPMorgan, Goldman Sachs, Apr 2026
delta: Surging — Apr 9 dual tariff shock: US 104% on China, China 84% on US
deltaNote: JPMorgan recession probability 65% (revised up Apr 9), Goldman Sachs 35%, Moody's Analytics ~50%; Iran ceasefire (Apr 7) briefly relieved oil shock (WTI -16.4% to $94.41) but China tariff escalation to 104%/84% on Apr 9 represents new acute escalation; FOMC Apr 29–30 critical
